title: Desk with Drawers and File Cubby Slots
summary: A pedestal-base desk combining euro-slide drawers with an open file cubby of vertical dividers — files stand upright and visible rather than lying flat in a drawer.
description:
This desk pairs a standard drawer pedestal with something most desks skip: an open cubby of vertical dividers sized to hold hanging files or folders standing upright, in view and reachable without pulling a drawer open at all. Build the pedestal box first exactly like a drawer cabinet — top, bottom, sides, and a center divider setting the boundary between the drawer bay and the cubby bay.

**The file cubby dividers are spaced and sized to the file-folder standard**, not to an arbitrary shelf spacing — this is what makes them actually useful for files rather than just another small shelf. Attach them evenly across the cubby bay before the desktop goes on, since the desktop's underside is what caps the top of each cubby slot.

Install the euro-slide drawers with the cabinet is level and square, then attach the desktop last, overhanging the pedestal on all visible sides for a finished look. Drawer faces get attached with an even reveal gap and pulls installed before mounting for easier drilling on a flat face.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut one desktop at 48" from the 3/4" cabinet-grade plywood, full sheets. Cut two pedestal top/bottom at 22", two pedestal sides at 28-1/4", one center divider at 28-1/4" and three file cubby dividers at 12" from the 3/4" cabinet-grade plywood, full sheets. Cut one back panel at 28-1/4" from the 1/4" plywood, full sheet. Cut six drawer sides at 18" and six drawer front/back at 16-1/2" from the 1x4. Cut three drawer bottoms at 18" from the 1/4" plywood, full sheet. Cut three drawer faces at 18-1/2" from the 1x2. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to three 8 ft 1x4 and one 8 ft 1x2. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Build the pedestal box
steps.body: Assemble the pedestal top, bottom, sides, and center divider with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, forming two bays: a drawer bay and a cubby bay.

steps.title: Add the file cubby dividers
steps.body: Space and attach the file cubby dividers evenly across the cubby bay, sized to file-folder standard spacing, before the desktop goes on.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Nail and glue the 1/4" plywood back panel to the pedestal.

steps.title: Build and install the drawers
steps.body: Build the drawer boxes with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, attaching bottoms with 3/4" brad nails and glue. Check the cabinet is level and install on euro drawer slides.

steps.title: Attach drawer faces and desktop
steps.body: Attach the drawer faces with an even reveal gap and install pulls. Attach the desktop to the pedestal, overhanging on the visible sides.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Pedestal desk with drawers and an open file cubby
